packages.name: moment
packages.reason: Moment.js is very large and no longer maintained. Consider a lightweight alternative.

packages.name: lodash
packages.reason: Import only specific functions (e.g. import get from 'lodash/get') or use native JS methods.

packages.name: axios
packages.reason: Native fetch is available in Node 18+ and modern browsers. Consider if axios is truly needed.

packages.name: jquery
packages.reason: jQuery is unnecessary in modern React/Next.js projects. Use native DOM APIs.

packages.name: bootstrap
packages.reason: In Next.js projects, CSS Modules or Tailwind are more performant and tree-shakeable.

packages.name: react-bootstrap
packages.reason: Heavy component library. Consider unstyled alternatives with Tailwind CSS.

packages.name: material-ui
packages.reason: MUI is very large. Import only the specific components you need.

packages.name: @mui/material
packages.reason: MUI is very large. Import only the specific components you need, or use Radix UI primitives.

packages.name: antd
packages.reason: Ant Design is extremely large. Use tree-shaking or consider a lighter UI library.

packages.name: redux
packages.reason: Redux itself is small but @reduxjs/toolkit adds significant overhead. Check if simpler state management suffices.

packages.name: @reduxjs/toolkit
packages.reason: Redux Toolkit is heavy. If your state needs are simple, consider Zustand or React Context.

packages.name: chart.js
packages.reason: Chart.js is large. For React/Next.js, consider react-chartjs-2 or a React-native library.

packages.name: three
packages.reason: Three.js is very large. Use dynamic import with next/dynamic to load it lazily.

packages.name: highlight.js
packages.reason: Highlight.js bundles all language grammars. Import only the languages you need.

packages.name: prismjs
packages.reason: Import only the specific Prism language modules you need to reduce bundle size.

packages.name: d3
packages.reason: D3 is very powerful but heavy. Import only specific D3 modules (e.g. d3-scale, d3-array).

packages.name: rxjs
packages.reason: RxJS is designed for Angular. In React/Next.js apps, native Promises and async/await are often sufficient.

packages.name: ramda
packages.reason: Ramda is large. Import only specific functions: import { map } from 'ramda/src/map'.

packages.name: faker
packages.reason: Faker should be a devDependency only. It should never be in production bundles.

packages.name: @faker-js/faker
packages.reason: Faker should be a devDependency only. It should never be in production bundles.

packages.name: underscore
packages.reason: Underscore.js is outdated. Use native JavaScript methods or a modern utility library.

packages.name: immutable
packages.reason: Immutable.js is heavy. Immer.js provides immutability with a fraction of the bundle size.

packages.name: styled-components
packages.reason: CSS-in-JS has runtime overhead. For Next.js, CSS Modules or Tailwind are faster.

packages.name: emotion
packages.reason: Emotion has a runtime cost. Consider CSS Modules for better performance.

packages.name: @emotion/react
packages.reason: Emotion has a runtime cost. Consider CSS Modules for better performance.

packages.name: framer-motion
packages.reason: Framer Motion is large. For simple animations, CSS transitions or auto-animate are much lighter.

packages.name: gsap
packages.reason: GSAP is powerful but heavy. Use native CSS animations for simple effects.

packages.name: quill
packages.reason: Quill is older and larger. Tiptap is a modern, extensible alternative.

packages.name: draft-js
packages.reason: Draft.js is deprecated by Meta. Migrate to Tiptap or Slate.

packages.name: crypto-js

alternatives.name: Web Crypto API
alternatives.description: Native browser crypto, zero bundle cost

packages.reason: Use the native Web Crypto API (crypto.subtle) available in all modern browsers and Node.js 16+.
